requirements.jd_requirement: Analyze AWS and GCP billing, usage, and cost trends to identify optimization opportunities.
requirements.evidence: Built and operated containerized services in AWS, including deep exposure to billing and usage patterns through ECS workloads.

requirements.jd_requirement: Implement cost-saving strategies, such as rightsizing, instance scheduling, reserved instances (RI), savings plans and Committed use discounts.
requirements.evidence: Automated infrastructure provisioning and lifecycle management using Terraform and CI/CD, enabling repeatable cost-aware resource deployments.

requirements.jd_requirement: Establish tagging policies, cost allocation mechanisms, and chargeback/showback models.
requirements.evidence: Built pipelines to reconcile inconsistent asset records across systems and normalize metadata for operational use — directly transferable to tagging and cost allocation.

requirements.jd_requirement: Optimize data transfer costs, storage lifecycle policies, and networking expenses.
requirements.evidence: Designed and maintained IaC for data pipelines with explicit attention to storage tiering, lifecycle policies, and egress cost control.

requirements.jd_requirement: Monitor spot instances, auto-scaling, and compute savings opportunities.
requirements.evidence: Operated auto-scaling Kubernetes workloads (EKS) and optimized compute allocation for security tooling, balancing cost and performance.

requirements.jd_requirement: Define and enforce FinOps best practices aligned with organizational objectives.
requirements.evidence: Established guardrails and runbooks for secure, repeatable infrastructure changes — same mindset applies to enforcing cost policies at scale.

requirements.jd_requirement: Collaborate with finance to forecast cloud expenses, create budgets, and track financial KPIs.
requirements.evidence: Built anomaly detection pipelines using Python to surface outliers in security telemetry — directly applicable to cloud cost forecasting and KPI tracking.

requirements.jd_requirement: Work with engineering teams to align architecture decisions with cost efficiency.
requirements.evidence: Translated security requirements into architectural guardrails and operational controls across engineering teams, balancing trade-offs between security, cost, and velocity.

requirements.jd_requirement: Implement automated cost reporting, anomaly detection, and alerting mechanisms.
requirements.evidence: Developed automated alerting for security anomalies using Python and Grafana; same pattern applies to cost anomaly detection.

requirements.jd_requirement: Utilize AWS Cost Explorer, AWS Budgets, AWS Trusted Advisor, AWS Cost & Usage Reports, GCP Billing Reports, Cost Tables, Committed Use Discounts Analysis, and Recommender API for cost insights.
requirements.evidence: Built IAM policies and cost-aware access controls in AWS/GCP; experienced with native cloud billing APIs for policy enforcement.
Built system-to-system automation across platforms using APIs — including internal tooling that ingested third-party data sources.

requirements.jd_requirement: Develop automation scripts using AWS Lambda, Python, Pulumi, or AWS SDKs to enforce cost policies.
requirements.evidence: Created Python-based automation for remediation and orchestration across AWS services, including Lambda functions for cost-aware security enforcement.

requirements.jd_requirement: Leverage FinOps dashboards and Tableau for visualization.
requirements.evidence: Designed dashboards for security telemetry using Grafana; adaptable to Tableau-style visualization needs.

requirements.jd_requirement: Work with our finance team to implement Continuous Accounting.
requirements.evidence: Translated finance and compliance requirements into technical controls for audit-ready systems — same approach applies to continuous accounting workflows.

tone_notes: Match Supabase’s builder-ops tone: grounded, pragmatic, and operationally focused. Emphasize shipping maintainable systems over theoretical perfection. Avoid hype; highlight cross-functional collaboration and measurable outcomes.
summary_angle: Position Conner as a security-native engineer who brings operational rigor, automation discipline, and data-driven cost awareness to FinOps — not just as a cost-cutter, but as someone who builds systems that make cost efficiency sustainable at scale.
